summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the LiteLLM proxy returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://evil-attacker.com and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true for preflight requests with an arbitrary Origin header, allowing any website to make credentialed cross-origin requests to the proxy.
expected_security_impact: A preflight OPTIONS request with Origin: http://evil-attacker.com should return Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://evil-attacker.com and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction installed litellm from the target repository, started the proxy, sent a preflight OPTIONS request with Origin: http://evil-attacker.com to /v1/chat/completions, and the response included Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://evil-attacker.com and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true along with Access-Control-Allow-Methods listing all HTTP methods and Access-Control-Allow-Headers including the requested headers.
verification_method: The verify.sh script sends an OPTIONS preflight request with Origin: http://evil-attacker.com and checks the response headers for Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://evil-attacker.com AND Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. Both must be present with the expected values.
evidence_produced_by_run: Response headers contain Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://evil-attacker.com, confirming the proxy echoes arbitrary origins.
Response headers contain Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true, confirming credentialed cross-origin requests are allowed from any origin.

Response headers contain Access-Control-Allow-Methods: DELETE, GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, PATCH, POST, PUT and Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, granting full access to the API from any origin.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
